# Mad Extra Bail Bonds — Full Business Profile

Licensed bail bondsman in Delray Beach, FL specializing in fast bail bond services across Palm Beach County, Broward County, and Miami-Dade County — available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.

- Primary Market: South Florida families facing bail emergencies

## Full About
Mad Extra Bail Bonds has served South Florida families for over 11 years from our base in Delray Beach, FL. We are a licensed Florida bail bondsman with deep experience posting bail across the county main jails, city lockups, and federal detention facilities in Palm Beach County, Broward County, and Miami-Dade County. We know the Palm Beach County Jail in West Palm Beach, Broward County Main Jail in Fort Lauderdale, Turner Guilford Knight Correctional Center (TGK) in Miami-Dade, and dozens of city and municipal facilities throughout South Florida.

We answer every call, 24 hours a day, 7 days a week — no answering machines, no voicemail traps. When we say available around the clock, we mean it: midnight, weekends, holidays, and every hour in between. Our experience with South Florida's jail systems means we know exactly who to contact and what to do, saving your family time during one of the most stressful moments imaginable.

Beyond posting bail, we go further than traditional bail bond services. We provide educational guidance on decision-making and proactive steps families can take to reduce future law enforcement encounters. Our goal is to help your family move forward — not just get through tonight.

## Service Details

### Bail Bond Services
General bail bond assistance for any criminal charge in South Florida. We work directly with the jail to verify the bail amount, complete the paperwork, and secure release as quickly as possible. For misdemeanor charges, same-day release is frequently achievable. Felony charges typically take 6-24 hours. Florida bail bond premium is set by state law at 10% of the court-ordered bail amount — non-refundable.

### DUI Bail Bonds
Immediate DUI bail bond assistance across South Florida. DUI cases in Florida often carry a mandatory 8-hour hold for first-time offenses before bail can be posted. We monitor these timelines and are ready to post the moment the hold clears. DUI with accident, injury, or prior DUI convictions may carry higher bail amounts and additional processing steps.

### Drug Charge Bail Bonds
Bail bonds for drug possession, trafficking, and related charges across Palm Beach County, Broward County, and Miami-Dade County. Drug charge bail amounts vary significantly by quantity, substance, and whether distribution is alleged. We handle these cases with complete confidentiality and move with urgency from the first call.

### Felony Bail Bonds
Felony charges carry higher bail amounts and require more experience to navigate. We are familiar with felony processing at every major South Florida facility. Felony release timelines are typically 6-24 hours after bond is posted. Our relationships with facility staff across the region allow us to move efficiently even during high-volume booking periods.

### Misdemeanor Bail Bonds
Fast, affordable bail bonds for misdemeanor charges in South Florida. Most misdemeanor releases happen within 2-6 hours of the bond being posted. Misdemeanor bail amounts are typically lower, but we handle every case with the same urgency regardless of charge level.

### Federal Bail Bonds
Federal bail bonds for defendants in federal court (Southern District of Florida). Federal cases are more complex — bail is not always guaranteed, amounts are typically higher, and conditions of release are often more stringent. We have experience with FDC Miami and the federal bail process in South Florida.

### Immigration Bail Bonds
ICE detention and immigration bail bonds for individuals held by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement. Immigration bonds are federally processed through the Department of Homeland Security. Two types: Delivery Bonds (for detainees with a deportation hearing) and Voluntary Departure Bonds. Processing typically takes 24-72 hours after bond is posted. We serve Miami-Dade's large immigrant community and surrounding areas.

### Warrant & Traffic Bonds
Outstanding warrants and traffic-related bonds handled quickly. An active warrant can result in arrest at any traffic stop or public encounter. Resolving a warrant proactively — before it escalates — is almost always the better path. We handle warrant bonds across all South Florida jurisdictions.

## Frequently Asked Questions

Q: What is a bail bond and how does it work in Florida?
A: A bail bond is a financial guarantee posted by a licensed bondsman on the defendant's behalf, promising the defendant will appear at all future court dates. In Florida, when a judge sets bail, you pay a bondsman 10% of that amount as a non-refundable premium. The bondsman posts the full bail amount with the court, securing release. The 10% fee is earned by the bondsman for assuming the financial risk.

Q: How much does a bail bond cost in South Florida?
A: The bail bond premium in Florida is set by state law at exactly 10% of the court-ordered bail amount. This rate is the same across Delray Beach, Fort Lauderdale, Miami, and every jurisdiction in our service area. The 10% fee is non-refundable and cannot be negotiated below the statutory rate.

Q: How long does it take to get someone released on a bail bond in South Florida?
A: Release timelines depend on the facility and charge type. Misdemeanor releases typically take 2-6 hours after bond is posted. Felony releases at Palm Beach County Jail or Broward Main can take 6-24 hours. Immigration and federal cases may take 24-72 hours. The earlier you call, the faster we can begin.

Q: What information do you need to start the bail process?
A: We need: the defendant's full legal name, date of birth, and Social Security number (or immigration ID); the name and location of the jail; the charges if known; and the bail amount if already set. If you don't have all of this, call anyway — we can help locate the defendant in the system.

Q: Do you offer payment plans for the bail bond premium?
A: We discuss payment arrangements on a case-by-case basis for qualifying circumstances. Call us and honestly describe your situation. We would rather work with your family to get your loved one home than turn you away over a financial obstacle.

Q: What happens if the defendant misses a court date?
A: If a defendant fails to appear, the judge issues a bench warrant for their arrest and the bail bond is forfeited. The bondsman is legally obligated to pay the full bail amount unless the defendant is located and returned within a specified period (typically 60 days in Florida). Call us immediately if a court date is missed — early action preserves options.

Q: Can you post bail for DUI, drug charges, and immigration cases?
A: Yes — we handle all charge types including DUI, drug possession and trafficking, felonies, misdemeanors, federal charges, immigration (ICE) detention, and outstanding warrants across all South Florida jurisdictions.

Q: What is an indemnitor (co-signer) and do I need one?
A: An indemnitor is a person who agrees to be financially responsible if the defendant fails to appear. Requirements vary by case — the bail amount, severity of charges, and the defendant's ties to the community all affect whether a co-signer is needed. We explain all requirements upfront before any paperwork is signed.

Q: What counties and facilities do you cover in South Florida?
A: We cover Palm Beach County (Palm Beach County Jail, all city lockups), Broward County (Broward County Main Jail, all city lockups), and Miami-Dade County (TGK Correctional Center, Metro West, all city lockups, federal detention). We are also licensed to post immigration bonds processed through DHS.

Q: Can you help if bail has been denied?
A: If a judge has denied bail (no-bond hold), no bondsman can post bail — only a defense attorney can petition the court for a bond hearing. We can explain the situation and help identify what steps are available. If bail has been set but not yet posted, call us immediately.

## Certifications & Trust Signals
- Licensed Florida Bail Bondsman (Florida Department of Financial Services)
- 11+ years serving South Florida families
- 24/7 availability — no answering machines, no voicemail
- Serving all South Florida jails: county, city, and federal
- Bilingual service available
- Payment arrangements available for qualifying cases
- Educational guidance on post-release next steps

### Broward County
Fort Lauderdale, Pompano Beach, Deerfield Beach, Coral Springs, Hollywood, Margate

### Miami-Dade County
Miami, Hialeah, Miami Gardens
